question: Will Sepp Straka win the 2026 Masters tournament?
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler's recent dominance, including victories at the Arnold Palmer Invitational and The Players Championship plus a runner-up at the Houston Open, anchors his 17.5% implied probability as traders' top pick for the Masters, bolstered by his elite ball-striking and strong Augusta finishes (T2 in 2023). Rory McIlroy trails at 8.5% amid driving prowess and eight top-10s this season, but iron inconsistency tempers grand slam hopes; Jon Rahm (7.2%) leverages defending champion status despite limited LIV prep, while Bryson DeChambeau (6.5%) and Ludvig Aberg (5.7%) excite with power and breakout form (Aberg's RSM Classic win). Differentiators include Augusta-specific metrics—approach play, short-game creativity, and major poise—amid a forecast of playable conditions and a deep field primed for upsets.

question: Will PH win the most seats in the 2026 Colombian Chamber of Representatives election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives.

If voting in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives election does not occur by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between this party and any other for the most seats won, this market will be resolved in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) at 99.5¢ in the Colombia Chamber of Representatives election, driven by official results from the July 20, 2024, internal vote where PH's candidate, Shadia Farah, secured the presidency with 93 votes amid coalition discipline and opposition abstentions. PH's plurality of seats from the 2022 legislative ballot, combined with President Petro's influence over allied blocs like Green Alliance and Polo Democrático, solidified this outcome in the 188-member chamber. Realistic challenges include procedural appeals or defections before final ratification, though minimal given the lopsided tally and precedent of coalition-led leadership rotations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election first round, with 70% implied probability reflecting perceived momentum from his Pacto Histórico affiliation and continuity appeal amid President Petro's agenda. Paloma Valencia trails at 18.1%, buoyed by Centro Democrático's right-wing base consolidation post-internal primaries. Recent drivers include Cepeda's strong performance in leftist pre-candidate surveys and Valencia's rising visibility in conservative media, while fragmented fields dilute others like Sergio Fajardo or Vicky Dávila. No recent polls show such dominance, underscoring market speculation on turnout and alliances ahead of March 2026 consultations; odds remain fluid amid economic pressures and security concerns.

Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Iván Cepeda Castro to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election first round, with 70% implied probability reflecting perceived momentum from his Pacto Histórico affiliation and continuity appeal amid President Petro's agenda. Paloma Valencia trails at 18.1%, buoyed by Centro Democrático's right-wing base consolidation post-internal primaries. Recent drivers include Cepeda's strong performance in leftist pre-candidate surveys and Valencia's rising visibility in conservative media, while fragmented fields dilute others like Sergio Fajardo or Vicky Dávila. No recent polls show such dominance, underscoring market speculation on turnout and alliances ahead of March 2026 consultations; odds remain fluid amid economic pressures and security concerns.

question: Will Vicky Dávila win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?

Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

events.title: Colombia Presidential Election
events.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors Miguel Uribe Turbay (49.5%) over Paloma Valencia (43.6%) in Colombia's 2026 presidential election, with Iván Cepeda Castro (40.5%) close behind, reflecting recent polls showing a fragmented right-wing field amid President Gustavo Petro's sagging approval ratings below 35%. The race remains tight due to undecided voters exceeding 40% in surveys, competing Centro Democrático aspirants splitting conservative support, and left-wing consolidation efforts post-Petro. Separation could emerge from CD primary outcomes, endorsements by ex-President Álvaro Uribe, fresh Invamer or Datexco polling, or national security/economic shifts before the May 2026 first round.

events.title: Will any presidential candidate win outright in the first round of the Colombia's election?
events.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any candidate wins this election in the first round. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the result of the first round of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from Invamer and Datexco in late 2024 show no Colombian presidential candidate surpassing 20% support ahead of the May 3, 2026, first-round vote, with leaders like Vicky Dávila, María Fernanda Cabal, and Gustavo Bolívar polling in the mid-teens amid a fragmented field following October primaries. Historical precedent reinforces trader consensus, as no candidate has won outright since 2002, typically forcing a June runoff between top two finishers. Absent major consolidations or endorsements shifting dynamics, the 94% implied probability on "No" reflects skepticism of any 50% threshold breach, with upcoming debates and surveys as key catalysts.

question: Will Liverpool finish in 2nd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool's four-point lead over Arsenal atop the Premier League table, with Manchester City just three points further back, underpins traders' 83% consensus on City securing second place, implying a probable title win for the Reds. City's recent four-match winning streak, fueled by Erling Haaland's scoring surge despite Rodri's ongoing absence, has closed the gap after early-season stumbles, while Arsenal's dropped points in draws against Everton and Brighton exposed vulnerabilities in their attack. United's 4.3% share reflects sporadic form under new manager Ruben Amorim, but lacks consistency for a top-two push; Villa's surge earns 1.8% on strong home results, yet faces a grueling run-in against title contenders. Momentum and depth favor City's overtake of Arsenal for runner-up.

events.title: English Premier League – 3rd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 3rd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United commands 62% trader consensus for finishing third in the Premier League, driven primarily by the transformative hiring of Ruben Amorim as head coach following Erik ten Hag's dismissal, sparking optimism for a second-half surge despite their current 11th-place standing on 19 points after 13 matches. Aston Villa holds 19.3% with solid early-season form under Unai Emery but recent draws exposing defensive frailties, while frontrunners Liverpool (31 points) at 8% reflect expectations they'll secure top-two rather than slip to third. Chelsea's four-game win streak under Enzo Maresca bolsters their 4% share amid City's injury-hit slump, with remaining fixtures, goal difference, and head-to-head clashes poised to shape the Champions League race.

events.title: English Premier League – Last Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in last place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolves dominate trader consensus for English Premier League last place at 56.3% implied probability after a historic winless start to 2024-25, rooted at the bottom with zero points from seven games, a -12 goal difference, and defensive collapses prompting Gary O'Neil's sacking and Vitor Pereira's appointment. Key absences like suspended Matheus Cunha and injured Yerson Mosquera exacerbate vulnerabilities ahead of tough fixtures against Arsenal and Manchester City. Burnley trails at 36.2%, reflecting lingering skepticism from their 2023-24 relegation with just 24 points, despite their current Championship lead under Scott Parker. Nottingham Forest and Leeds see low odds (0.8% each) buoyed by mid-table form and promotion pushes, while West Ham and Tottenham's attacking depth renders basement finishes improbable.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on EPL relegation heavily favors the promoted trio—Ipswich Town, Southampton, and Leicester City—with implied probabilities above 50% for each after dismal starts, including Southampton's zero wins in eight matches prompting manager Russell Martin's sacking. Ipswich sits bottom with two points and a league-worst goal difference of -13, while Leicester lingers in 18th despite a slight uptick under new boss Steve Cooper. Recent draws for Wolves and Everton have eased their peril, but all face grueling November fixtures against top-half sides, where historical data shows bottom-table teams win just 15% of such games. Squad depth gaps and mounting injury lists, like Leicester's absent key midfielders, amplify downside risks amid the wisdom of crowds pricing in survival odds.

If the listed club is officially relegated by the English Premier League following the 2025–26 season, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on EPL relegation heavily favors the promoted trio—Ipswich Town, Southampton, and Leicester City—with implied probabilities above 50% for each after dismal starts, including Southampton's zero wins in eight matches prompting manager Russell Martin's sacking. Ipswich sits bottom with two points and a league-worst goal difference of -13, while Leicester lingers in 18th despite a slight uptick under new boss Steve Cooper. Recent draws for Wolves and Everton have eased their peril, but all face grueling November fixtures against top-half sides, where historical data shows bottom-table teams win just 15% of such games. Squad depth gaps and mounting injury lists, like Leicester's absent key midfielders, amplify downside risks amid the wisdom of crowds pricing in survival odds.

question: Will GPT-6 be released before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI's GPT-6 model is made available to the general public before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

To qualify, GPT-6 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by OpenAI as being accessible to the general public.

GPT-6 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-6 (e.g. ChatGPT-6o would count), or one that is recognized as a successor to GPT-5, similar to the progression from GPT-4 to GPT-5. Products labeled as GPT-5.5 or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for the release of GPT-6 will be official information from OpenAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "What will happen before GTA VI?" market tilts toward high-probability outcomes like major trailer drops or rival game launches, driven by Rockstar's reaffirmed Fall 2025 console release during Take-Two's May earnings call, implying a narrow window for preceding events. Recent hype from the December 2023 trailer's 200 million views underscores massive anticipation, but historical delays—like GTA V's slip from 2013—fuel skepticism on exact timing. Key watchpoints include Take-Two's November earnings for delay signals and potential Trailer 2 reveals at The Game Awards in December, which could shift odds if they confirm or push back the date; streaming metrics and pre-order buzz will further calibrate implied probabilities amid volatile gamer sentiment.

question: Will Wrexham be promoted to the EPL?
description:
This is a polymarket on whether Wrexham A.F.C. will be promoted to the English Premier League (EPL) for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the current English football league season.

If Wrexham is officially promoted to the EPL for the 2026–27 season, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Official promotion means Wrexham has secured a place in the EPL for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Championship, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by the English Football League (EFL) and the Premier League.

If this season is canceled or not completed by July 1, 2026, with no official promotion decisions made for the 2026–27 season, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from the Premier League and the English Football League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Wrexham be promoted to the EPL?

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' heavy lean toward "No" at 85% implied probability stems from Wrexham's position in EFL League One, requiring consecutive promotions—first automatic top-two finish there, then in the Championship—to reach the Premier League by 2026-27, a rare feat historically achieved by few clubs like Southampton in recent decades. Despite a blistering start with eight wins in their first 11 League One matches, topping the table ahead of powerhouses like Birmingham City and Huddersfield Town, skepticism persists amid a grueling schedule featuring derbies and promotion rivals. No major injuries plague stars like Paul Mullin (12 goals) or Elliot Lee, but rest disadvantages from midweek cups and the "second season" fatigue in League One justify the wisdom of crowds pricing in substantial upset risk.

question: Will FC Augsburg be relegated from the Bundesliga after the 2025–26 season?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs are relegated from the Bundesliga after the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club is officially relegated by the Bundesliga following the 2025–26 season, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Holstein Kiel leads the Bundesliga relegation peril, mired at the bottom with 8 points from 14 matches and a league-worst goal difference of -20, fueling trader consensus for their likely drop. Bochum sits precariously one spot above in 17th on 10 points but gained crucial momentum via a shock 3-2 home win over Bayern Munich on Matchday 14. Wolfsburg (16th, 13 points) holds the playoff position amid shaky form. Upcoming fixtures intensify pressure: Bochum hosts RB Leipzig, Kiel visits Stuttgart, and all face schedule rigor against top-half sides. Recent results underscore volatility, with no bottom team safe given historical late surges in the two-horse drop and playoff race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Angers SCO anchors the Ligue 1 relegation zone at 18th with just 8 points from 15 matches, their porous defense conceding 32 goals fueling trader consensus at over 90% implied probability of drop, while Montpellier (17th, 13 points) and Le Havre (16th, 14 points) cling to survival hopes amid a congested bottom six separated by mere goal difference. Recent form shows Montpellier's gritty 1-1 draw at Nantes boosting momentum, but Le Havre's home woes persist after a 2-0 loss to Rennes; Saint-Étienne and Auxerre hover dangerously close post-promotion struggles. Upcoming fixtures intensify pressure—Angers faces Lille away, Montpellier hosts PSG—with winter transfers and potential managerial shifts like Angers' coaching carousel pivotal for escaping the drop.

eventMetadata.context_description: Angers SCO anchors the Ligue 1 relegation zone at 18th with just 8 points from 15 matches, their porous defense conceding 32 goals fueling trader consensus at over 90% implied probability of drop, while Montpellier (17th, 13 points) and Le Havre (16th, 14 points) cling to survival hopes amid a congested bottom six separated by mere goal difference. Recent form shows Montpellier's gritty 1-1 draw at Nantes boosting momentum, but Le Havre's home woes persist after a 2-0 loss to Rennes; Saint-Étienne and Auxerre hover dangerously close post-promotion struggles. Upcoming fixtures intensify pressure—Angers faces Lille away, Montpellier hosts PSG—with winter transfers and potential managerial shifts like Angers' coaching carousel pivotal for escaping the drop.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Erling Haaland's dominant 88% implied probability stems from his league-leading 11 goals through 11 matches, fueled by Manchester City's fluid attack and his elite 28% shot conversion rate, far outpacing rivals. Recent hat-trick versus West Ham and full recovery from early-season ankle injury have solidified trader consensus, reflecting City's historical firepower under Pep Guardiola. Challengers like Mohamed Salah (8 goals) or Ollie Watkins face steeper paths amid tougher fixtures and lower xG totals. Realistic disruptions include Haaland injury recurrence, City's midweek Champions League demands, or a prolonged drought akin to his 2023 dip, though City's depth mitigates some risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappe's overwhelming 92.8% implied probability as La Liga top goalscorer reflects his elite finishing and Real Madrid's high-powered attack, amplified by a recent hat-trick in a 5-0 rout of Real Betis that boosted his tally to 9 goals through 13 matches. Traders favor the 25-year-old Frenchman over 36-year-old leader Robert Lewandowski (12 goals) due to Mbappe's youth, speed, and central role in Madrid's title chase, contrasting Barcelona's occasional inconsistencies. Supporting this, Madrid's favorable remaining fixtures and Mbappe's penalty duties edge out challengers like Lamine Yamal or Vinicius Junior. Realistic threats include Mbappe injury, Lewandowski's clinical streak persisting, or Barcelona surging amid their strong start.
